analysis.reasoning: Core message: EU presidents are signing a declaration launching the Conference on the Future of Europe, enabling citizen participation in reshaping EU policies and institutions. EU1: Not explicitly about past successes or overcoming history; neutral institutional update, so not promoted. EU2: Does not emphasize unity or agreement among member states; no solidarity framing, so not promoted. EU3: Yes—highlights a process designed to involve citizens in shaping policies/institutions, implying usefulness and responsiveness to citizens’ concerns within the EU. EU4: Somewhat suggests institutional innovation, but no explicit pioneering/regulatory leadership or forward-looking policy breakthroughs; evidence is weak, so not promoted. EU5: No reference to global human rights/democracy promotion abroad. EU6: No claim about improving the world or external partnerships. Conclusion: Only EU3 is supported.
